proof fn phi_1_create_key_namespace_disagrees_with_obj(req: CreateRequest)
    requires
        req.obj.metadata.namespace is Some,
        req.namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
    ensures
        req.key().namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
{
}
reason: CreateRequest::key uses self.namespace rather than self.obj.metadata.namespace, so the key can silently disagree with the object's own namespace metadata

name: unmarshal_ignores_state_validation
code:
proof fn phi_3_unmarshal_ignores_state_validation(obj: DynamicObjectView)
    requires
        obj.kind == VReplicaSetView::kind(),
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(obj.spec) is Ok,
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_status(obj.status) is Ok,
        !(VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(obj)->Ok_0).state_validation(),
    ensures
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(obj) is Ok,
{
}
reason: unmarshal succeeds without checking state_validation, allowing structurally invalid objects to enter the system through deserialization

name: marshal_spec_no_injectivity
code:
proof fn phi_4_marshal_spec_no_injectivity(s1: VReplicaSetSpecView, s2: VReplicaSetSpecView)
    requires
        s1 != s2,
        VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(s1) == VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(s2),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: marshal_spec is uninterpreted with no injectivity axiom, so two distinct specs could serialize to the same Value — the system cannot distinguish different configurations after storage

name: object_ref_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_ignores_uid(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v2.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v1.metadata.name == v2.metadata.name,
        v1.metadata.namespace == v2.metadata.namespace,
        v1.metadata.uid != v2.metadata.uid,
    ensures
        v1.object_ref() == v2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: object_ref uses only kind/name/namespace and ignores uid, so a deleted-and-recreated resource with a new uid maps to the same ObjectRef — stale reconciliation could target the wrong instance
